title: 'A Cure for Wellness' Director Gore Verbinski on Making Something Outside the Box and the Future of the Industry
format: Sean Fennessey talks to Gore Verbinski the week 'A Cure for Wellness' opens: building the spa's horror out of old castles and a Berlin hospital, why the mid-budget movie is disappearing, and how 'Rango' and the 'Pirates' trilogy got made.

films.title: A Cure for Wellness
films.note: Verbinski on a health-spa horror he calls 'not immediately reducible': Dane DeHaan's Lockhart summoned to a wellness castle in the Alps that treats diagnosis as absolution. Shot across castles in Germany, Austria, Switzerland, the Czech Republic and Romania, with interiors in a Berlin-area hospital where Hitler was treated for mustard gas after WWI. A stage fire burned the set down and cost six weeks.

films.title: Mouse Hunt
films.note: His first feature; he says he overly storyboarded it and left 40 minutes on the editing-room floor.

films.title: The Mexican
films.note: The follow-up, where he swung the other way and kept it loose. He now thinks he stopped working on the script too early.

films.title: The Ring
films.note: Cited as the scale he finds cathartic to return to after big-IP tentpoles; a 'Rings' sequel opens the same year as this interview.

films.title: Pirates of the Caribbean: The Curse of the Black Pearl
films.note: The first Pirates, greenlit on the premise that a pirate movie 'will never work,' which is exactly what excited him.

films.title: Pirates of the Caribbean: Dead Man's Chest
films.note: The 'second pirate movie'; what scared him was that the studio wasn't nervous anymore, telling him to 'just keep doing that thing you're doing.'

films.title: Pirates of the Caribbean: At World's End
films.note: Closing out his trilogy; three Pirates felt like the right journey before there was no more personal growth left in it.

films.title: Rango
films.note: The Oscar-winning animated film he made 'startup'-style: six artists in his ranch house, 18 months on a story reel recorded on a Mac, final animation at ILM, none of whom had made an animated movie. Sean calls it a top-10 film of all time.

films.title: The Lone Ranger
films.note: Referenced as another massive production; he describes managing a set the size of Pirates or this without going brusque or loud.
